skill.name: global-coding-style
skill.description: Apply consistent coding standards for PHP, TypeScript, and JavaScript projects, including formatting, naming, typing, linting, and review preparation.
skill.summary: Guides consistent PHP and TypeScript code style, type safety, cleanup, and automated quality checks.
skill.author: DevanB

security_audit.summary: Static path traversal alerts are confirmed as an external relative documentation reference in SKILL.md, not executable code. This creates a filesystem access and hidden dependency risk because the skill directs the assistant outside its package. The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts are false positives from prose; no cryptographic code or algorithm use was found.

medium_findings.title: External Relative File Reference
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md directs the assistant to ../../../agent-os/standards/global/coding-style.md. This is a confirmed path traversal pattern that asks the assistant to consult a file outside the skill package. It appears to be a standards reference, not malicious code, but it creates filesystem access risk and an undeclared dependency.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The traversal sequence is explicit and repeated in a relative path on line 30. The context indicates a documentation reference, so confidence in filesystem risk is high but confidence in malicious intent is low.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Alerts
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptographic algorithm alerts at SKILL.md lines 3 and 8 occur in descriptive prose. No evidence found of cryptographic functions, weak algorithms, executable scripts, or code that handles secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file contains Markdown guidance only. The flagged lines do not include cryptographic code or algorithm names in an executable context.

dangerous_patterns.title: Path Traversal Sequence in Skill Reference
dangerous_patterns.description: The ../../../ sequence on line 30 points outside the skill directory. This pattern can cause an assistant to read files that are not packaged with the skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The relative traversal sequence is directly present in the file. The risk is constrained because it references a documentation path rather than code execution.

content.user_title: Standardize PHP and TypeScript Code Style
content.value_statement: Inconsistent formatting, naming, and typing slow reviews and create avoidable defects. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to apply shared PHP and TypeScript standards.
actual_capabilities: Guides PHP formatting with Laravel Pint conventions.
Guides TypeScript and JavaScript formatting with Ultracite conventions.
Encourages explicit PHP return types and parameter type hints.
Promotes TypeScript type usage in frontend code.
Supports naming cleanup, dead code removal, and DRY refactoring.
Prepares code for review with linting and static analysis expectations.
limitations: The referenced detailed standards file is outside the skill package.
It provides style guidance, not a runnable formatter or linter.
It does not define complete rules for every framework or language feature.
Tool commands depend on the target project configuration.

use_cases.title: Clean Up Backend Changes
use_cases.description: Apply PHP type declarations, formatting expectations, naming cleanup, and static analysis readiness before review.

use_cases.title: Standardize Frontend Code
use_cases.description: Align TypeScript and JavaScript edits with consistent typing, linting, and formatting expectations.

use_cases.title: Prepare Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Remove dead code, reduce duplication, and check style expectations before submitting a change.

prompt_templates.title: Format a Small Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a focused style pass.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this PHP or TypeScript change for naming, formatting, type usage, and dead code. Keep behavior unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Add Missing Types
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for type safety improvements.
prompt_templates.prompt: Update these functions to use explicit PHP return types or stronger TypeScript types. Explain any ambiguous type choices.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request before opening a pull request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Check this branch for style issues, unused imports, duplicated logic, and likely linter or static analysis failures.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor to Standards
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a broader cleanup pass.
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this module to follow project coding standards, reduce duplication, improve names, and preserve public behavior.

output_examples.input: A PHP service method has loose parameter types and no return type.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies missing type declarations, suggests explicit parameter and return types, and notes any behavior-sensitive uncertainty.

output_examples.input: A TypeScript component contains unused imports and inconsistent names.
output_examples.output: The assistant proposes import cleanup, clearer names, stronger type usage, and a formatter or linter check.

output_examples.input: A pull request includes repeated helper logic across files.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends a shared helper only when it reduces meaningful duplication and preserves readability.

best_practices: Run the project formatter and linter after applying style changes.
Keep refactors behavior-preserving unless the task asks for functional changes.
Confirm ambiguous type choices with nearby code and project conventions.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new abstractions only to satisfy style preferences.
Do not remove code unless it is clearly unused or intentionally replaced.
Do not apply formatter assumptions that conflict with the project configuration.

faq.question: Does this skill run Laravel Pint or Ultracite?
faq.answer: No. It gives guidance for using those tools when they are available in the project.

faq.question: Can it change application behavior?
faq.answer: The skill is intended for style, typing, cleanup, and review preparation, not behavior changes.

faq.question: Does it support both backend and frontend code?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers PHP plus TypeScript and JavaScript style expectations.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The skill references a relative path outside its package, which creates filesystem access and packaging concerns.

faq.question: Is the weak cryptography alert confirmed?
faq.answer: No. The flagged lines are prose, and no cryptographic implementation was found.

faq.question: What should publishers fix before release?
faq.answer: They should include the referenced standards file inside the skill package or remove the external relative path.
